If '÷' means addition, '+' means subtraction, '×' means division and '−' means multiplication, then which of the following equations is not correct?

यदि '÷' का अर्थ जोड़ है, '+' का अर्थ घटाव है, '×' का अर्थ भाग है और '−' का अर्थ गुणनफल है, तो निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा समीकरण सही नहीं होगा?

question:
Who was the first Indian Governor-General of independent India?

स्वतंत्र भारत के पहले भारतीय गवर्नर-जनरल कौन थे?
option_1: Lord Mountbatten (लॉर्ड माउंटबैटन)
option_2: C. Rajagopalachari (सी. राजगोपालाचारी)
option_3: Jawaharlal Nehru (जवाहरलाल नेहरू)
option_4: Dr. Rajendra Prasad (डॉ. राजेंद्र प्रसाद)
solution_text:
After India gained independence in 1947, Lord Mountbatten served as the Governor-General until June 1948. After him, C. Rajagopalachari became the first and only Indian Governor-General of independent India.

भारत को 1947 में स्वतंत्रता मिलने के बाद लॉर्ड माउंटबैटन जून 1948 तक गवर्नर-जनरल रहे। उनके बाद सी. राजगोपालाचारी स्वतंत्र भारत के पहले और एकमात्र भारतीय गवर्नर-जनरल बने।

option_3: 2 and 3 only (केवल 2 और 3)

option_3: 1 and 3 only (केवल 1 और 3)

question:
If x + y + z = 0, then find the value of .

यदि x + y + z = 0, तो का मान ज्ञात कीजिए।

A milkman mixes water in pure milk and offers the mixture for sale at the original cost price of milk. If he mixes 0.25 liters of water in every 6.50 liters of milk and sells the entire mixture at the price of pure milk, what is his profit percentage?

एक दूधवाला शुद्ध दूध में पानी मिलाकर उस मिश्रण को दूध के मूल क्रय मूल्य पर बेचता है। यदि वह प्रत्येक 6.50 लीटर दूध में 0.25 लीटर पानी मिलाता है और पूरे मिश्रण को शुद्ध दूध के मूल्य पर बेचता है, तो उसका लाभ प्रतिशत क्या होगा?
option_1: 2.5%
option_2: 3.84%
option_3: 6.66%
option_4: 7.64%

question:
The ratio of the speed of a boat to that of the current water is 35 : 8. The boat goes along with the current in 5 hours 10 minutes. What will be the time taken by the boat to come back?

नाव की चाल और जलधारा की चाल का अनुपात 35 : 8 है। नाव धारा के साथ 5 घंटे 10 मिनट में जाती है। वापसी में नाव को कितना समय लगेगा?
option_1: 5 hours 15 minutes 58 seconds
option_2: 6 hours 45 minutes 10 seconds
option_3: 8 hours 13 minutes 42 seconds
option_4: 9 hours 30 minutes 49 seconds

question:
If (sin x + sin y) = a and (cos x + cos y) = b, then find the value of (sin x sin y + cos x cos y).

यदि (sin x + sin y) = a तथा (cos x + cos y) = b हो, तो (sin x sin y + cos x cos y) का मान ज्ञात कीजिए।

If secθ + tanθ = x, then find sinθ.

यदि secθ + tanθ = x हो, तो sinθ ज्ञात कीजिए।

A right circular cone having height of 15 cm is cut by two parallel planes at heights 5 cm and 10 cm from the base. What is the ratio of the volumes of the three parts (from top to bottom)?

15 सेमी ऊँचाई वाला एक समवृत्त शंकु आधार से 5 सेमी और 10 सेमी की ऊँचाई पर दो समांतर समतलों द्वारा काटा जाता है। ऊपर से नीचे तक बने तीन भागों के आयतनों का अनुपात क्या है?
option_1: 8 : 19 : 27
option_2: 1 : 6 : 20
option_3: 8 : 19 : 64
option_4: 1 : 7 : 19

question:
Select the word that is similar in meaning (SYNONYM) to the word given below.

RAPACIOUSNESS
solution_text: Rapaciousness means a strong desire to take or grab things greedily. Voraciousness also means having an extreme desire to get or consume more and more, so it matches the meaning. Profusion means a large quantity, not greed. Munificence means generosity, which is the opposite. Beneficence means doing good for others, which is not related.

question:
Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph.

A. The shopman was very interested in selling this creamy box to her as he would gain a big margin.

B. The man on the counter showed her an exquisite little enamel box with a very fine glaze as if baked in cream.

C. As the box was too expensive, priced twenty-eight guineas, she asked the shopkeeper to keep it for her.

D. She went inside a little antique shop in Curzon Street. This was the usual shop where the shopkeeper was ridiculously fond of serving her.
solution_text: The paragraph starts with the woman entering the shop, so sentence D comes first. Then the shopkeeper shows her the box, so B follows. After that, his eagerness to sell is explained in sentence A. Finally, she asks him to keep the box because it is expensive, so C comes last. Other options do not follow a logical time order.

question:
Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice.

The invigilator is advising the students not to carry calculators into the examination hall.
option_1: The students are being advised by the invigilator not to carry calculators into the examination hall.
option_2: The students have been advised not to carry calculators into the examination hall by the invigilator.
option_3: The students were advised not to carry calculators into the examination hall.
option_4: The students were advised the invigilator not to carry calculators into the examination hall.
solution_text: The sentence is in present continuous tense. In passive voice, present continuous becomes “are being + past participle”. Option 1 follows this rule correctly. Option 2 changes the tense to present perfect. Option 3 changes the tense to past. Option 4 is grammatically incorrect.

question:
Select the word that is similar in meaning (SYNONYM) to the word given below.

Her steadfast commitment to the cause was quite admirable.
solution_text: Steadfast means firm and not changing. Unwavering has the same meaning, so it is correct. Stubborn means refusing to change even when wrong. Tolerant means accepting others’ views. Flexible means able to change easily. These do not match the meaning of steadfast.

question:
In the following question, out of the four alternatives, select the word opposite in meaning to the word given.

Meretricious
solution_text: Meretricious means attractive on the surface but lacking real value. Natural means real and not artificial, so it is the opposite. Brazen means shamelessly bold. Exemplary means very good or ideal. Gaudy means overly bright and showy. These are not opposites of meretricious.

question:
Choose the correct option to transform as directed.

The clerk at the booking window inquired if I wanted a single or a return ticket.

(Change to Direct speech)
option_1: The clerk at the booking window said, "I wanted a single or return ticket?"
option_2: "Do you want a single or a return ticket?" asked the clerk at the booking window.
option_3: The clerk asked, "What you want to book, a single or a return?"
option_4: The booking clerk asked me, "Single or return, please?"
solution_text: The given sentence is in indirect speech. To change it into direct speech, the reporting verb “inquired” is changed to “asked”. The word “if” is removed and the question is written inside quotation marks. The pronoun “I” changes to “you”. Option 2 follows all these rules correctly. The other options are either grammatically incorrect or do not properly form a direct question.

question:
The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.

Children listens to people / speaking around them / for a long time before / they begin to speak.
option_1: Children listens to people
option_2: speaking around them
option_3: they begin to speak
option_4: for a long time before
solution_text: The subject “Children” is plural, so it must take a plural verb. The verb “listens” is singular and incorrect here. It should be “listen”. The other parts of the sentence are grammatically correct. Therefore, the error is in option 1.

question:
Select the word which means the same as the group of words given.

A situation in which many people leave a place at the same time
solution_text: Exodus means a mass departure of people from a place, which exactly matches the given meaning. Mitigation means reducing the severity of something. Navigation means finding and following a route. Immigration means coming to live in another country. Hence, option 3 is correct.

question:
Convert the following active voice sentence into passive voice.

The committee members accepted the new policy proposal with unanimous consent.
option_1: The new policy proposal was accepted by the committee members with unanimous consent.
option_2: With unanimous consent, the committee members have accepted the new policy proposal.
option_3: The new policy proposal is accepted with unanimous consent by the committee members.
option_4: By the committee members, the new policy proposal has been accepted with unanimous consent.
solution_text: In passive voice, the object of the active sentence becomes the subject. Here, “the new policy proposal” becomes the subject. The correct structure is “was accepted by the committee members”. Option 1 follows the correct tense and structure. The other options either change the tense or are incorrectly framed.

question:
Improve the underlined part of the sentence.

Harsha is the tallest than all the other boys in his colony.
option_1: no substitution
option_2: than some other boys
option_3: of all the boys
option_4: than any boy
solution_text: When using a superlative form like “tallest”, we use “of all” to show comparison with the entire group. “Of all the boys” correctly shows that Harsha is taller than every boy in the colony. The other options use incorrect comparison words. Therefore, option 3 is correct.

question:
Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined phrase.

Though very rich, Alexander always saves for a rainy day.
option_1: Saves money for later
option_2: Lives like a miser
option_3: Spends too much
option_4: Accumulates less wealth
solution_text: The phrase “saves for a rainy day” means saving money for future needs or emergencies. It shows careful planning for difficult times. Option 1 directly matches this meaning. Option 2 suggests extreme miserliness, which is different. Option 3 is the opposite meaning. Option 4 talks about gaining less wealth, not saving money.

question:
Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.

Someone who studies elections and voting statistics
solution_text: A psephologist is a person who studies elections, voting patterns, and voting data. This exactly matches the given meaning. Analyst is a general term for someone who studies data. Psychologist studies human behavior. Mathematician studies mathematics. Hence, option 4 is correct.

question:
In the following sentence, select the most appropriate option to fill the blank.

All of my efforts to save her life have gone in ______.
solution_text: The word “vain” means without success or result, which fits the sentence correctly. Vein refers to blood vessels. Van is a vehicle. Vane is a blade moved by wind or water. Therefore, option 1 is correct.

question:
Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.

Be like chalk and cheese
option_1: To be completely different from each other
option_2: To be very similar to each other
option_3: To have a close relation with another person
option_4: To be distantly related to each other
solution_text: Chalk and cheese are totally different things. The idiom means two people or things that have no similarity at all. Option 1 correctly explains this meaning. The other options do not match the idea of complete difference.

question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
solution_text: The correct spelling is “Repulsive”. The word “Repulcive” is incorrectly spelt. Nocturnal, Scoreboard, and Irrespective are correctly spelt words. Therefore, option 2 is the correct answer.

question:
Fill up the blank with the correct homophone.

Pilgrims stayed at _______ in the past.
option_4: None of these
solution_text: An inn is a place that provides food and lodging to travellers. Inns is the plural form of inn, which fits the sentence correctly. Ins and in do not have this meaning. Therefore, option 1 is correct.

question:
Select the option which best expresses the given sentence in Indirect speech.

The cab driver said to me, “I was waiting for you for a long time.”
option_1: The cab driver said to me that he was waiting for me for a long time.
option_2: The cab driver told to me that he is waiting for me for a long time.
option_3: The cab driver told me that he has been for me for a long time.
option_4: The cab driver told me that he had been waiting for me for a long time.
solution_text: The sentence is in direct speech. When converting to indirect speech, the reporting verb is in the past tense, so the tense of the reported speech also changes. “I” changes to “he” and past continuous changes to past perfect continuous. Option 4 correctly follows all these rules. The other options have tense or grammatical errors.

question:
The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.

Scarcely had I / started reading / the paper / then the doorbell rang.
option_1: Scarcely had I
option_4: then the doorbell rang
solution_text: The word “scarcely” is always followed by “when” and not “then”. Therefore, the phrase “then the doorbell rang” is incorrect. The correct structure is “Scarcely had I started reading the paper when the doorbell rang”. Hence, option 4 contains the error.

question:
In the question below, a part of the sentence is underlined. Below are given alternatives to the underlined part which may improve the sentence. Choose the correct alternative.

We should take up the first item, let us begin this song.
option_1: this song begin us
option_2: us begin with this song
option_3: begin the song
solution_text: The correct structure is “let us begin with”. Option 2 uses the correct verb form and preposition. Option 1 and option 3 are grammatically incorrect. Option 4 is incorrect because the sentence needs improvement. Hence, option 2 is correct.

question:
Following passage consists of six sentences. The first sentence (S1) and the final sentence (S6) are given. The middle four sentences in each have been removed and jumbled up. These are labelled P, Q, R and S. You are required to find out the proper sequence of the four sentences.

S1: Our age is the age of the specialist.

P: Modern specialization has led to the fragmentation of knowledge.

Q: Each one knows more and more about less and less.

R: We should not only be specialists but also have a sense of the meaning of life and of social responsibility.

S: We concentrate on some narrow field and forget the larger context in which we can see the meaning of our own specialization.

S6: We have to reckon with the spirit of science, understand its limitations and an outlook which is consistent with its findings.
solution_text: Sentence P explains the effect of specialization mentioned in S1. Sentence Q further explains how knowledge becomes limited. Sentence S adds that we forget the larger meaning of our work. Sentence R concludes by stressing the need for social responsibility along with specialization. Therefore, the correct order is PQSR.

question:
In the following passage, some of the words have been left out, each of which is indicated by a letter. Find the suitable word from the options given against each letter and fill up the blanks with appropriate words to make the paragraph meaningful.

The 'normal' monsoon forecast of the India Meteorological Department brings the promise of a year of growth and good health for India's economy and ___(A)___ If correct, India will have a second consecutive year of normal rainfall, atter two years of ____(B)____. The prospect that 2017 will be a good year boosts the prospects of enhanced agricultural output, healthy ____(C)____. levels, more hydropower and reduced conflicts over water. It will also test the ____(D)____ of the expensive water management initiatives launched during 2014 and 2015 by the Centre and the State governments to harness rainfall and build resilience for future drought cycles. As the IMD's experience shows, forecasting the all-India summer monsoon rainfall is fraught with ____(E)____ and has often gone off the mark.

Which of the following should be filled in the place of (A)?
solution_text: The sentence talks about growth and good health for India’s economy and the environment. Ecology fits best because it relates to living things and their environment. Prosperity does not fit well because health is already mentioned. Atmosphere only refers to mood or air. Bio-diversity does not match the overall context.

question:
In the following passage, some of the words have been left out, each of which is indicated by a letter. Find the suitable word from the options given against each letter and fill up the blanks with appropriate words to make the paragraph meaningful.

The 'normal' monsoon forecast of the India Meteorological Department brings the promise of a year of growth and good health for India's economy and ___(A)___ If correct, India will have a second consecutive year of normal rainfall, atter two years of ____(B)____. The prospect that 2017 will be a good year boosts the prospects of enhanced agricultural output, healthy ____(C)____. levels, more hydropower and reduced conflicts over water. It will also test the ____(D)____ of the expensive water management initiatives launched during 2014 and 2015 by the Centre and the State governments to harness rainfall and build resilience for future drought cycles. As the IMD's experience shows, forecasting the all-India summer monsoon rainfall is fraught with ____(E)____ and has often gone off the mark.

Which of the following should be filled in the place of (B)?
solution_text: The sentence refers to rainfall and monsoon conditions. Drought means a long period of low rainfall, which fits perfectly here. Hunger and calamity are general terms. Scarcity is too broad and does not specifically relate to rainfall.

question:
In the following passage, some of the words have been left out, each of which is indicated by a letter. Find the suitable word from the options given against each letter and fill up the blanks with appropriate words to make the paragraph meaningful.

The 'normal' monsoon forecast of the India Meteorological Department brings the promise of a year of growth and good health for India's economy and ___(A)___ If correct, India will have a second consecutive year of normal rainfall, atter two years of ____(B)____. The prospect that 2017 will be a good year boosts the prospects of enhanced agricultural output, healthy ____(C)____. levels, more hydropower and reduced conflicts over water. It will also test the ____(D)____ of the expensive water management initiatives launched during 2014 and 2015 by the Centre and the State governments to harness rainfall and build resilience for future drought cycles. As the IMD's experience shows, forecasting the all-India summer monsoon rainfall is fraught with ____(E)____ and has often gone off the mark.

What should be filled in the place of (C)?
solution_text: The sentence talks about enhanced agricultural output, hydropower, and water availability. Reservoir levels fit this context well. Yield would repeat the idea of agricultural output. Rainfall and monsoon are already discussed earlier and would be repetitive.

question:
In the following passage, some of the words have been left out, each of which is indicated by a letter. Find the suitable word from the options given against each letter and fill up the blanks with appropriate words to make the paragraph meaningful.

The 'normal' monsoon forecast of the India Meteorological Department brings the promise of a year of growth and good health for India's economy and ___(A)___ If correct, India will have a second consecutive year of normal rainfall, atter two years of ____(B)____. The prospect that 2017 will be a good year boosts the prospects of enhanced agricultural output, healthy ____(C)____. levels, more hydropower and reduced conflicts over water. It will also test the ____(D)____ of the expensive water management initiatives launched during 2014 and 2015 by the Centre and the State governments to harness rainfall and build resilience for future drought cycles. As the IMD's experience shows, forecasting the all-India summer monsoon rainfall is fraught with ____(E)____ and has often gone off the mark.

Which of the following should be filled in the place of (D)?
solution_text: The sentence talks about testing the success of water management initiatives. Efficacy means the ability to produce the desired result, which fits perfectly. Reality gives a negative tone. Responsibility and synergy do not match the meaning.

question:
In the following passage, some of the words have been left out, each of which is indicated by a letter. Find the suitable word from the options given against each letter and fill up the blanks with appropriate words to make the paragraph meaningful.

The 'normal' monsoon forecast of the India Meteorological Department brings the promise of a year of growth and good health for India's economy and ___(A)___ If correct, India will have a second consecutive year of normal rainfall, atter two years of ____(B)____. The prospect that 2017 will be a good year boosts the prospects of enhanced agricultural output, healthy ____(C)____. levels, more hydropower and reduced conflicts over water. It will also test the ____(D)____ of the expensive water management initiatives launched during 2014 and 2015 by the Centre and the State governments to harness rainfall and build resilience for future drought cycles. As the IMD's experience shows, forecasting the all-India summer monsoon rainfall is fraught with ____(E)____ and has often gone off the mark.

Which of the following should be used in the place of (E)?
solution_text: The line says the forecast has often gone off the mark, which shows unpredictability. Uncertainties correctly expresses this idea. Examples, instances, and events do not convey unpredictability. Hence, option 1 is correct.
